name: Robert Is Here
reasoningTrace:
Resolved Robert Is Here → robertishere.com.
Fetched site — legendary Homestead fruit stand, mamey shake fame.
Hours and seasonal fruit list present but visually dated.
Mobile: phone number exists but not clickable in cached HTML.
Trust: family story is buried — huge missed emotional hook.
Conversion: no clear 'Get directions from Miami' for day-trippers.
Overall 45/100 — beloved brand, site feels stuck in 2008.

snapshot.title: Robert Is Here Fruit Stand | Homestead FL
snapshot.description: Famous South Florida fruit stand. Fresh tropical fruit, shakes, and farm market.
snapshot.rawText: Robert Is Here fruit stand in Homestead Florida near Everglades entrance. Famous mamey and key lime shakes. Fresh tropical fruit in season: mango, lychee, dragon fruit. Family owned since 1959. Open daily seasonal hours. Call (305) 246-1590 for availability.

clarity.reason: Fruit stand identity is there but the famous shakes and Everglades trip context aren't in the hero.

trust.reason: Family-since-1959 story exists on an inner page — should lead the homepage.

mobile.reason: Phone isn't tap-to-call and photos aren't optimized — day-trippers are on phones in the car.

speed.reason: Unoptimized images and table layout likely feel sluggish on rural LTE near Homestead.

conversion.reason: No directions CTA for Miami visitors — they Google instead of clicking through.

localSeo.reason: Homestead and Everglades in copy but not combined in one searchable headline.

topFixes: Hero: 'Famous mamey shakes on the road to the Everglades since 1959.'
Tap-to-call + Google Maps directions as dual mobile buttons.
Show what's in season this week above the fold.
